file_name: PlayboyEnterprisesInc_20090220_10-QA_EX-10.2_4091580_EX-10.2_Content License Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Sales-Purchase Agreement2.txt

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Contract may not be modified or terminated except in writing, and no claimed modification, termination or waiver shall be binding unless also signed by an authorized representative of Playboy.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Contract may be modified or terminated verbally, and no claimed modification, termination or waiver shall be binding unless also signed by an authorized representative of Supplier.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing verbal modifications and terminations of the contract. This directly contradicts the original statement that modifications and terminations must be in writing. Also, by requiring that the modifications, terminations or waivers must be signed by an authorized representative of Supplier and not Playboy, this is again another contraddiction against the original term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Contract may be modified or terminated verbally, and no claimed modification, termination or waiver shall be binding unless also signed by an authorized representative of Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the contract may be modified or terminated verbally, directly opposing the initial requirement for written modification or termination. The original text states 'This Contract may not be modified or terminated except in writing', while the changed text explicitly allows verbal modifications. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the permissible methods for modifying or terminating the contract. Furthermore, changing the signatory requirement from Playboy to Supplier causes another contradiction.
